bead.title: pre_claim_intake decoder rejects readiness_checks.verdict=bool; entire f: part 1
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-f9ddaa68 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 1 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the same warn-only rejection, so the worker logs a warning and skips. With 23 ready beads in the fizeau queue and one fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue is idle indefinitely.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC; about an hour after the previous wedge bug ddx-8f2e0ebf)
- Worker: PID 1527216, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 01:22:40 at observation.
- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779077845655338391.jsonl
- Pattern every ~50s: resource.preflight (status ok) -> pre_claim_intake.start -> pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle.
- Sample pre_claim_intake.warn payload:
  {
    "bead_id":"fizeau-5b2a8950",
    "decision":"warn",
    "decision_source":"pre_claim_intake",
    "detail":"decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string",
    "fingerprint":"15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f",
    "outcome":"intake_error",
    "policy_mode":"warn-only",
    "reason":"system_unready",
    "rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready",
    "suggested_action":"check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry",
    "system_reason":"unavailable"
  }
- Identical fingerprint across multiple bead_ids confirms it is a schema mismatch, not a bead-specific issue.
- Queue state at observation: open=44, ready=23, dependency-waiting=19. None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.
- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.
2. warn-only policy_mode masks the failure: the worker keeps polling and looks alive (loop.idle every 50s) but never claims anything. Operator has no signal beyond tailing the agent-loop log.
3. The 'suggested_action' text ('check the
...[truncated]

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- pre_claim_intake decoder accepts readiness_checks.verdict as JSON bool, JSON string, or absent; coerces to a documented canonical form. Unit test covers all three. Verified by a focused decoder test in the pre-claim intake package.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
bead.acceptance:
1. pre_claim_intake decoder accepts readiness_checks.verdict as JSON bool, JSON string, or absent; coerces to a documented canonical form. Unit test covers all three. Verified by a focused decoder test in the pre-claim intake package.
2. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
3. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"decision":"warn","decision_source":"pre_claim_intake","detail":"readiness route unavailable: lifecycle dispatch: project root mutation rejected (changed existing dirty paths: .ddx/beads.jsonl)","fingerprint":"e050f98fd02ceb82376ad67098871ad3c20e9b729038d4a1358c77a0d9132996","policy_mode":"warn-only","reason":"system_unready","rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready","suggested_action":"revise the rewrite so it preserves every explicit commitment","warning_fingerprint":"e050f98fd02ceb82376ad67098871ad3c20e9b729038d4a1358c77a0d9132996"}
